query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell values. Here's the summary of findings:

## Key Findings

**Latest weekly/daily data (early July 2026):**
- **July 1, 2026:** Foreign net sell of **1.70 trillion won** — the 9th consecutive selling session
- The recent trend has been consistently negative (net selling) in trillions of won, accelerating sharply in late June

**Record-breaking figures:**
- **June 29:** Record single-day net sell of **~7.73 trillion won** (largest since 2001)
- **June 2026:** Record monthly net sell of **~48.62 trillion won**
- **May 2026:** 47.19 trillion won net sell (previously the record month)
- **H1 2026 cumulative:** **~148–149 trillion won** ($95–107B) — roughly **10× the entire full-year 2025** figure of 11.07 trillion won, and 6–8× larger than the 2008/2020 crisis outflows

**Context/drivers:**
- Selling is framed as **profit-taking and rebalancing** after the KOSPI's ~101% H1 surge, not a loss of confidence (per KRX CEO)
- A weakening won (USD/KRW hit ~1,554.9 on July 1, a 17-year high) created a negative feedback loop
- Selling concentrated in semiconductors (Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix)
- **Paradox:** Despite record selling, foreign ownership ratio *rose* to record highs (~40.7% of KOSPI) because valuation gains outpaced outflows
- Retail investors (+99T won) and institutions (+35T won) absorbed the selling

The full report with all data points and sources has been submitted via the structured output.

key_facts: July 1, 2026 (latest daily): foreign investors net sold 1.7029 trillion won on KOSPI, the 9th consecutive session of net selling; KOSPI fell 2.04% to 8,303.41.
June 29, 2026: record single-day foreign net sell of ~7.73 trillion won (7.7331-7.756T cited), largest since data began September 2001.
June 2026: record monthly foreign net sell of ~48.62 trillion won (48.6248T), the largest monthly outflow in history.
May 2026: foreign net sell of 47.19 trillion won across all listed stocks (KOSPI -49.41T, KOSDAQ +2.22T); 5th straight month of net selling.
H1 2026 KOSPI foreign net selling: ~148.3 trillion won (Yonhap/Infomax); ~149.0-149.5 trillion won ($95-107B) on Korea Exchange basis — a record, 6-8x the 2008/2020 crisis outflows.
Full-year 2025 foreign net selling was only 11.07 trillion won; H1 2026 is roughly 10x that.
Offsetting buyers in H1 2026: retail individuals +99.174 trillion won; institutions +35.045 trillion won.
KOSPI surged ~101% in H1 2026 (up 108.85% YTD before June 3 selloff); selling framed as rebalancing/profit-taking, not loss of confidence.
USD/KRW reached ~1,554.9 on July 1, 2026, a 17-year-3-month high, creating a feedback loop driving further foreign selling.
Despite record selling, foreign ownership of KOSPI rose to ~40.7-40.93% (from 36.65% at year-start); FSS reported 35.3% of total market cap, holdings 2,852.3 trillion won.
Selling concentrated in semiconductors: Samsung Electronics net sell ~20.77 trillion won and SK Hynix ~19.71 trillion won in June.
June 23, 2026: foreign net sell of ~5.80 trillion won during a 9.99% KOSPI crash that triggered a circuit breaker.
Brokerages (Korea Investment, Samsung, KB, Kiwoom) expect net selling to continue into H2 2026, while raising KOSPI targets to 11,000-11,500.
